comments.body:
No actionable comments were generated in the recent review. 🎉
ℹ️ Recent review info
⚙️ Run configuration **Configuration used**: defaults **Review profile**: CHILL **Plan**: Pro Plus **Run ID**: `16e54806-56f7-4fa5-8528-f56dcdc07b9f`
📥 Commits Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between 363b84a494634c22d6198194cfd2d203d713d3ac and 50a714168a9d60625a5049d71f5487bfedf72905.
📒 Files selected for processing (1) * `packages/client/src/hooks/useTrainFeatures.ts`
🚧 Files skipped from review as they are similar to previous changes (1) * packages/client/src/hooks/useTrainFeatures.ts
---
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Adds a shared idle-scheduler and uses it to defer shape-index construction and to preload inactive transit-mode routes/stops during idle time, with cancellation guards and cache re-checks to avoid races. ## Changes **Idle Scheduling and Background Preloading** |Layer / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Idle Scheduler Utility**
`packages/client/src/lib/scheduleIdle.ts`|New module exports `scheduleIdle(cb, timeoutMs?)` returning a numeric handle for `requestIdleCallback` or `setTimeout` fallback, and `cancelIdle(handle)` to cancel work.| |**Extract Idle Scheduler from TrackInterpolation**
`packages/client/src/lib/trackInterpolation.ts`|Replaces inline idle-scheduler logic with `scheduleIdle` import; preserves prewarm scheduling behavior.| |**Defer Shape Index Computation**
`packages/client/src/hooks/useTrainFeatures.ts`|Defers `buildShapeIndex(routeShapes)` to idle time, clears index on entry, cancels pending work, and adds `shapeIndexVersion` to trigger downstream effects once build completes; updates dependent effect dependencies and adds early-return guards.| |**Background Route/Stop Preloading**
`packages/client/src/App.tsx`|New effect on `transitMode` change schedules an idle-time (~1500ms) preload of the inactive mode’s routes and stops only when missing in cache; re-checks cache before setting and cancels on cleanup.| ## Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant User participant App as AppComponent participant Idle as scheduleIdle participant Cache as modeCache participant API as fetchRoutes/Stops User->>App: change transitMode App->>Idle: schedule background preload (~1500ms) Idle->>Cache: check cache for inactive mode alt cache miss Cache->>API: fetch routes/stops API->>Cache: re-check and setRoutes/setStops else cache hit Cache-->>App: skip fetch end App->>Idle: cancelIdle(handle) on unmount/change ``` ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 3 (Moderate) | ⏱️ ~20 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - [cjunks94/panoptrain#30](https://github.com/cjunks94/panoptrain/pull/30): Edits to trackInterpolation idle scheduling and prewarm logic overlap with this PR. - [cjunks94/panoptrain#36](https://github.com/cjunks94/panoptrain/pull/36): Changes to useTrainFeatures and App-level mode handling touch the same code paths. - [cjunks94/panoptrain#54](https://github.com/cjunks94/panoptrain/pull/54): Introduces/uses a shared scheduleIdle and modifies client-side interpolation idle scheduling similarly. ## Poem > 🐰 In idle whispers, tasks rehearse, > Routes and shapes queue up, not worse. > While users drag and maps unfold, > We quietly fetch what days will hold. > A patient rabbit preloads gold.
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 4 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 50.00% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (4 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title 'perf(ui): preload inactive mode + defer shape-index build' directly and clearly summarizes the two main performance optimizations in the changeset: background preloading of inactive transit mode data and deferred shape-index construction. | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
📝 Generate docstrings - [ ] Create stacked PR - [ ] Commit on current branch
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `perf/preload-inactive-mode`

reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 2**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/client/src/hooks/useTrainFeatures.ts`:
- Around line 83-94: The deferred shape-index build currently writes
shapeIndexRef.current after scheduling with scheduleIdle, but the
data-processing effect (which computes trackPaths via processSlice) snapshots
the ref and never re-runs when the idle task completes; fix this by clearing
shapeIndexRef.current when routeShapes changes, track a small "indexVersion"
state (or ref) that is incremented after buildShapeIndex(routeShapes) finishes
inside the scheduled callback, and add that indexVersion as a dependency to the
effect that computes trackPaths so it re-runs when the deferred index lands;
modify the useEffect using scheduleIdle/cancelIdle (and the callback that sets
shapeIndexRef.current) to increment indexVersion after assignment so
processSlice sees the populated index.

In `@packages/client/src/lib/scheduleIdle.ts`:
- Around line 14-18: The fallback in scheduleIdle currently calls setTimeout(cb,
1) and ignores the timeoutMs parameter; update scheduleIdle so that when
hasIdleApi is false it calls setTimeout(cb, timeoutMs) (using the provided
timeoutMs) and still returns a numeric handle, ensuring requestIdleCallback is
used when hasIdleApi is true and the fallback honors the timeoutMs value;
reference function scheduleIdle, variable hasIdleApi,
globalThis.requestIdleCallback and the setTimeout fallback.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🪄 Autofix (Beta)</summary>

</details>
